title: crotalaria medicaginea var. neglecta
intro: Crotalaria medicaginea var. neglecta, also known as Australian rattlebox, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family and is native to Australia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Crotalaria medicaginea var. neglecta include its potential to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant may include teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety for human consumption.
